pattern: Digit DP over reachable remainders lets a greedy left-to-right digit choice build the largest palindrome divisible by k without ever trying a candidate number directly.
hints: Only the first half of the palindrome's digits are free choices (the second half mirrors them, plus one shared middle digit when n is odd), and each half-position contributes a fixed weight (a power of 10, or a sum of two powers of 10) to the total value mod k.

Precompute, for every suffix of half-positions, the set of remainders mod k that suffix could possibly contribute (there are at most k of them); this tells you whether a given digit choice at the current position can still be completed into a divisible number.

Greedily pick each half-digit from 9 down to 0 (1 down to 0 excluded for the leading digit), accepting the first digit whose required remaining remainder is in the precomputed achievable set for the rest of the half; mirror the finished half to form the answer.

edge_inputs.description: four-digit palindrome forcing the leading digit below 9
single digit, k=9 forces the digit down from 9
three-digit palindrome divisible by 4, needs even last-digit parity reasoning

stress.description: n at its maximum with a representative k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    k = rng.randint(1, 9)
    return [n, k]

stress.description: moderately large n across all k values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    k = ((size + rng.randint(0, 8)) % 9) + 1
    return [n, k]
